title: Helianthopsis microphylla
intro: Helianthopsis microphylla, also known as the 'small-flowered Helianthopsis', is a plant species in the family Gentianaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa, due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may aid in treating various infections and inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Helianthopsis microphylla include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to treat fever, sore throats, and digestive issues.
